title: Fullstack Next.js 14 Application Authentication with NextAuth
description: Standard approach for securing an entire Next.js App Router application leveraging server actions, iron-session, or NextAuth (Auth.js) securely.
steps: 1. Install `next-auth@beta` for Next.js 14.
2. Create the `auth.ts` configuration file exporting auth, handlers, signIn, signOut.
3. Create the route handler in `app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts`.
4. Setup a DB adapter (e.g. PrismaAdapter) if using Magic Links or Credentials.
5. Protect specific routes using Middleware (matching routes like `/dashboard/:path*`).
6. Retrieve session dynamically in Server Components using `await auth()`.
7. Use `<SessionProvider>` only if accessing session frequently on the Client.

title: OAuth and Magic Links for React + Node.js/Express Backend
description: Building a custom authentication system for a separated React SPA and Express Backend using Passport.js or a third-party service like Clerk/Supabase.
steps: 1. Decide whether to build natively (Passport.js/JWT) or use a managed service (Clerk/Supabase).
2. For managed: Install Clerk SDKs on both frontend (`@clerk/clerk-react`) and backend (`@clerk/clerk-sdk-node`).
3. Wrap React App with `<ClerkProvider>`.
4. Protect React routes using `<SignedIn>` and `<SignedOut>` components.
5. On the Express backend, apply the Clerk `requireAuth` middleware to restricted endpoints.
6. Pass the Authorization header containing the specific active session token from React.

title: CI/CD Pipeline Setup for Frontend Apps via GitHub Actions
description: A workflow for building, testing, and deploying frontend apps automatically.
steps: 1. Create a `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` file.
2. Define triggers: `on: push: branches: [ main ]`.
3. Set up the environment using `actions/setup-node`.
4. Add a step to run `npm ci` and `npm test`.
5. Add a step to run `npm run build`.
6. Use an action like `peaceiris/actions-gh-pages` or explicitly deploy to Vercel/Netlify via their CLI integrations.

title: Designing a Scalable Design System in Figma
description: Workflow for designing, testing, and scaling a fully-fledged design system using Figma variables and auto-layout.
steps: 1. Establish foundational tokens (colors, typography scales, spacing variables).
2. Create atomic components (buttons, inputs) heavily utilizing Auto Layout.
3. Build variants for interactive states (hover, active, disabled).
4. Leverage Figma's Component Properties to reduce structural duplication.
5. Create larger organism components combining atomic pieces.
6. Publish the library and document usage heavily.

title: End-to-end Testing with Playwright
description: Process for writing and executing robust, resilient E2E tests for web applications.
steps: 1. Install Playwright via `npm init playwright@latest`.
2. Configure `playwright.config.ts` targeting necessary browsers and base URLs.
3. Write tests focusing on user-flow rather than implementation details.
4. Utilize Playwright's Codegen feature to quickly map out user steps.
5. Run tests locally and debug using the `--ui` or `--debug` flags.
6. Integrate Playwright into your CI/CD pipeline.
